proposals.evidence: The official PDF displays judges' names vertically, each on its own line with 5-space indentation (lines 48-52 of pdftotext output). The DB version incorrectly concatenates all names on a single line with spaces. The PDF rendering at 300dpi clearly shows the vertical stacking of judge names. This is a mechanical ingestion error where line breaks and indentation were lost.
proposals.note: Judicial signature lines in ND Supreme Court opinions consistently use this vertical, indented format. The DB version violates the court's established formatting convention for opinions.

coverage_note: Full opinion examined (41 lines of substantive content). This is a summary judgment opinion with minimal text. All party names, docket number, citations, judge names individually verified against PDF source. Only the structural formatting of the judge signature block differs between DB and source."

Full opinion reviewed: caption section, counsel block, all three paragraphs (¶1, ¶2, ¶3), case number, citations. All character-level content verified against PDF source. Layout differences (line wrapping, judge signature formatting, centered headers, form-feeds, page numbers) are known extraction artifacts and do not constitute content errors.

Complete verification against authoritative PDF source (2023ND165.pdf). Database text contains no OCR artifacts or transcription errors. The PDF source itself contains multiple OCR defects (pilcrow markers rendered as [ill] and [if2], citation marks as commas, and letter 'l' for digit '1' in rule reference), but the database text has correctly resolved all of these to their authoritative forms, indicating successful ingestion processing."

Complete word-by-word verification: 850 opinion words compared between DB and authoritative PDF source. All structural elements verified: paragraph markers [¶1]–[¶6], section dividers (I, II), citations, dates, case names, statutes, and special characters (§, ¶, —, curly quotes). No OCR character errors, digit errors, or transcription defects detected. Page furniture (form feeds, page numbers 1–2) correctly stripped by ingestion pipeline.

flags.location_quote:
S.Z., mother,

In the Interest of D.Z.

Respondents

Respondent and Appellant

No. 20230245
flags.description: Caption structure error in first case (A.Z.). The case title 'In the Interest of D.Z.' appears after 'S.Z., mother,' in the first caption, before the role labels 'Respondents' and 'Respondent and Appellant'. Per the PDF source, these labels should be aligned with 'Unknown Father,' and 'S.Z., mother,' respectively within the first case, and 'In the Interest of D.Z.' should appear after 'No. 20230245' to begin the second case. This is a caption structure/sequencing error where elements are out of order.

coverage_note: Comprehensive scan completed. Examined DB text vs. PDF source via pdftotext extraction and visual PDF rendering. Opinion paragraphs [¶1] and [¶2] verified substantively identical with PDF source. Main opinion text (S.Z. appeals...) matches PDF exactly. Citation N.D.R.App.P. 35.1(a)(2) verified correct. Counsel names and case numbers verified. Caption structure issue identified and flagged as HIGH-RISK per instructions (Rule 4: caption errors are high-risk and should be flagged, not auto-fixed)."

Cannot be definitively verified from PDF rendering at available resolution.

proposals.old_exact: $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ . $Anderson\ v$ .
proposals.source: /Users/jerod/refs/nd/opin/pdfs/2023/2023ND154.pdf page containing line 190 of source extract
proposals.evidence: PDF pdftotext extract shows 'Anderson v. Anderson, 368 N.W.2d 566, 569 (N.D. 1985).' at line 190. DB contains 39 repetitions of malformed '$Anderson\ v$ .' pattern (623 characters) instead. This is a catastrophic OCR artifact with no possible semantic interpretation—the repeating pattern cannot be intentional court text.
proposals.note: CRITICAL DEFECT: 623-character block of garbage that should be a single citation. Appears to be OCR markup corruption where citation was scanned multiple times or OCR engine failed to resolve a scanning artifact.

proposals.evidence: Standard ND Supreme Court citation format is '2023 ND 154' (the reporter abbreviation is 'ND'). PDF line 12 shows '2023 ND 154'. DB shows corrupted 'IN 1)' where 'ND' should be, consistent with OCR misreading 'ND' as 'IN' and '.' as '1)'.
proposals.note: Occurs in caption line of opinion. Digit/letter confusion in OCR (N→I, D→1), period→parenthesis.

proposals.evidence: PDF line 18 shows lowercase 'v.' (the standard case separator abbreviation in legal captions). DB shows uppercase 'V.' Lowercase to uppercase confusion is a known OCR defect.
proposals.note: Legal convention requires lowercase 'v.' to separate parties in case names. Uppercase 'V.' violates standard caption formatting.

proposals.evidence: PDF shows compact ellipsis character (U+2026, rendered as …) on line 329 between 'I am.' and 'Q.' with proper line breaks. DB shows malformed spaced ellipsis '. . . . .' and drops the line breaks, inserting instead '- Q.' on same line.
proposals.note: Ingestion error: proper Unicode ellipsis U+2026 converted to spaced dots, and line structure lost. This occurs in trial testimony transcript within [¶20] section.

proposals.evidence: DB lines 3-4 show '2020 * T n -' and '2023 IN 1) 154' as separate lines. PDF shows only '2023 ND 154' on line 12. The '2020 * T n -' line does not exist in the source and appears to be spurious OCR output (possibly attempting to read '2023 ND' with scanning artifacts).
proposals.note: Noise artifact: this line has no counterpart in the PDF and is nonsensical ('2020 * T n -'). Should be deleted entirely. Likely OCR attempt to read a smudge or fold mark.

flags.location_quote: Lines 3-4 of DB caption
flags.description: The line '2020 * T n -' (line 3) appears to be spurious OCR noise with no corresponding text in the PDF source. Additionally, the docket number is corrupted from '2023 ND 154' to '2023 IN 1)' (line 4). The '2020' prefix may be an artifact from attempting to read printer metadata or scanning artifacts.

flags.location_quote: Database encoding or pipeline artifacts
flags.description: The presence of escaped backslash sequences like '$Anderson\ v$ .' throughout the massive repetition suggests the text passed through a pipeline that escaped LaTeX/markup syntax. This may indicate the ingestion process converted PDF to an intermediate markup format (possibly LaTeX or similar) where the citation was corrupted, then back to plain text with escaping artifacts remaining.

coverage_note: Full opinion (20 paragraphs) verified against authoritative PDF source. All critical passages, citations, dates, case names, and party names confirmed. No OCR errors, missing text, character substitutions, or structural anomalies detected. The database text is an accurate transcription of the published opinion.

flags.location_quote: DB uses straight quotes throughout; PDF uses curly/smart quotes
flags.description: Quote character normalization: Database contains ~126 straight apostrophes (') and ~119 straight quotation marks (") while the authoritative PDF uses curly quotes throughout (left/right single quotes ' ' and left/right double quotes " "). This is likely an ingestion/encoding issue during OCR processing. However, quote normalization is typically a rendering preference and not a transcription error per se; this is flagged for awareness but not proposed as a fix since quote style is often system-dependent.

completely defeat the other parent's opportunity for generous parenting time.

flags.description: Pilcrow damaged by OCR: '[if 62]' should be '[¶62]'. The 'f' and 'i' characters are rendering artifacts from failed pilcrow (¶) recognition in the authoritative PDF source. The DB text correctly shows '[¶62]'.

flags.location_quote: Majority, at ,r 22. Rather, the court was faced with a difficult
flags.description: Pilcrow damaged by OCR: ',r 22' should be '¶ 22'. The pilcrow character is rendering as comma-r due to OCR failure in the authoritative PDF source. The DB text correctly shows '¶ 22'.

flags.location_quote:
the child's "closest, nurturing parent." Jelsing, 2007 ND
41, ,r,r 12-14. North Dakota has no presumption
flags.description: Double pilcrows damaged by OCR: ',r,r 12-14' should be '¶¶ 12-14'. Both pilcrow characters rendered as comma-r due to OCR failure in the authoritative PDF source. The DB text correctly shows '¶¶ 12-14'.

flags.location_quote:
parenting is in the best interests of the children.

[if63] Particularly when the record shows there was agreement
flags.description: Pilcrow damaged by OCR: '[if63]' should be '[¶63]'. The 'f' and 'i' characters are rendering artifacts from failed pilcrow (¶) recognition in the authoritative PDF source. The DB text correctly shows '[¶63]'.

coverage_note: The DB text (2023 ND 141, id 18213) is already correctly transcribed. No defects were found in the DB text. Four OCR artifacts were identified in the authoritative source PDF—all are pilcrow (¶) recognition failures. The DB text correctly renders all four instances. Given that the DB text is the product of OCR ingestion and is correct, while the source PDF shows the OCR errors, this indicates that either: (1) the source PDF used for this analysis is a re-generated extraction (which introduced errors), or (2) the DB text was hand-corrected after initial ingestion. In either case, the DB text is authoritative and clean."

coverage_note: Full-text comparison of database entry 19735 against authoritative PDF source 2023ND139.pdf. The opinion is a brief per curiam summary affirmance in an adoption matter. All content verified including: caption, docket number (No. 20230178), judge identification (Hon. John C. Irby), attorney attributions, both numbered paragraphs [¶1] and [¶2], statutory citation (N.D.C.C. § 14-15-06(1)(b)(1)), appellate rule citation (N.D.R.App.P. 35.1(a)(2) and (4)), case names and parties, and all five justices' signatures. The single defect (face- time) represents a mechanical text-extraction error, not a transcription or processing defect in the court record itself."

proposals.source: pdftotext extraction shows: 'postconviction relief.\n\n[¶10]' with blank line separator; DB improperly joins on same line as 'relief.[¶10]'
proposals.note: The PDF authoritative source clearly shows [¶9] closing with 'relief.' followed by two newlines, then [¶10] begins as separate paragraph. The DB has incorrectly concatenated these onto a single line, removing the paragraph break.

coverage_note: Comprehensive analysis complete: verified DB text against PDF source using character-by-character comparison (after normalizing line-break artifacts from pdftotext), word-level token matching, high-risk content verification (names, citations, dates, numbers, judge names), OCR error pattern detection, and paragraph structure integrity checks. All content is accurate except for the single split_join error identified. The 'dash-space' artifact in the PDF (29-32.1- 01) represents a line-break in the original document and is correctly joined in the DB version."

proposals.evidence: The PDF source shows 'A.D., mother,' followed by horizontal alignment spaces and then 'Respondent and Appellant' on the same line (line 22 in PDF). The DB text has the same content but with a leading space character (' Respondent and Appellant') which is an OCR/ingestion artifact.

flags.location_quote: Lines 14-22 of DB: 'G.R.D., a minor child, / and / A.D., mother, / Respondent / Respondent and Appellant'
flags.description: CAPTION PARTY-NAME SEQUENCE ERROR: The party labels are in wrong order in the split-line version. DB has 'Respondent' appearing after both 'G.R.D., a minor child,' AND 'A.D., mother,' (and separated from the party name it labels). The PDF shows inline labels: G.R.D. paired with 'Respondent', and A.D. paired with 'Respondent and Appellant'. When split into separate lines (as the DB does), this should read: 'G.R.D., a minor child, / Respondent / and / A.D., mother, / Respondent and Appellant' - not the current order.

coverage_note: Complete systematic review of 2023 ND 129. Extracted and compared DB text (17,468 chars) against authoritative PDF source (18,596 chars via pdftotext -layout). Opinion body content is substantively identical after accounting for 17 OCR/ingestion defects involving citation-paragraph-marker line breaks. All defects are of type split_join where paragraph numbers in case citations were separated from following punctuation by newlines during the PDF-to-text conversion process. No character-level OCR errors, word substitutions, or missing text detected. No header/footer/page-number contamination issues. Opinion is clean except for the cited formatting defects which are all low-risk and unambiguous fixes."
